name: Typographic Hierarchy
description: Reference for the typography classes used across the FlowX designer, with rules for composing page, section, and subsection titles with descriptions.

roles.name: Page Title
roles.usage: The single page-level heading in the top title card. One per page.

roles.name: Section Title
roles.usage: Title of a major section inside the body card (Filters, Entries, General Settings, and similar).

roles.name: Subsection Title
roles.usage: Title of a labelled subsection or grouping inside a section.

roles.name: Description
roles.usage: Short supporting copy paired with any title (page, section, or subsection). Same style at every level; the surrounding title sets the hierarchy.

supportingStyles.name: Field Label
supportingStyles.note: Used in the composition examples for form field labels; not one of the four documented hierarchy roles.

rules: Every page uses exactly one Page Title.
Sections sit below the Page Title.
Subsections group fields inside a section.
Pair any title with a Description only when extra context is needed.
The Description style is identical at every level; the title above it is what establishes the hierarchy level.

cardShell.note: This shell is how the hierarchy page illustrates its examples and predates the Cards pattern. When building real screens, card containers follow patterns/cards.json (16px radius, Primary shadow / Secondary border) — use this pattern only for the text roles and spacing rules.

structure.pageHeaderCard: Bordered card containing the Page Title with its Description, 24px padding, 6px gap between title and description.
structure.sectionCard: Bordered card with a Section Title + Description block (6px gap) followed by content, 24px padding, 16px gap between title block and content.
structure.subsectionCard: Same card shell as a section card but titled with Subsection Title; nests inside a section card.
structure.buttonRow: Right-aligned Cancel (secondary) and Save (primary, var(--flowx-blue-500)) buttons, 8px gap, placed at the bottom of the card that owns the form.

scenarios.name: Scenario A
scenarios.rule: One section with a subsection inside: page header card, then a section card that nests a subsection card holding the form fields and the button row.

scenarios.name: Scenario B
scenarios.rule: Sibling section cards directly under the page title: each section gets its own bordered card; the button row lives in the last section card.

scenarios.name: Scenario C
scenarios.rule: Sibling sections sharing the same card: one bordered card contains multiple Section Title blocks stacked with 24px gaps, with a single button row at the bottom of the shared card.
